headline: T-Mobile scales back a longtime customer perk amid struggles
summary: T-Mobile is limiting a longtime perk it has advertised for years, despite recently facing elevated customer losses. The move is part of a broader series of changes it has made to its customer perks and benefits in recent months. For example, shortly after T-Mobile appointed Srini Gopalan as ...

headline: A Look At T-Mobile US (TMUS) Valuation After Recent Share Price Swings
summary: Context for T-Mobile US stock after recent moves T-Mobile US (TMUS) has drawn fresh attention after recent share price swings, with the stock down about 6% over the past month but higher over the past 3 months and week. See our latest analysis for T-Mobile US. The recent 6.3% 30 day share price decline comes after a 6.4% gain over 90 days, while the 1 year total shareholder return of 23.1% decline contrasts with solid 3 and 5 year total returns. If T-Mobile US has you reassessing your next...

headline: Deutsche Telekom shares slip after T-Mobile merger talks reports
summary: Deutsche Telekom shares fell 1.5% on Wednesday on reports of potential merger talks between the German ‌telecoms conglomerate and U.S.-based T-Mobile US, which would be the ‌largest ever public merger if it goes ahead.  Telekom, which already holds a majority 53% stake in T-Mobile, did not respond to a request for comment on Wednesday morning.  Bloomberg first reported ‌the potential deal.

content.title: Deutsche Telekom shares slip after T-Mobile merger talks reports

Telekom, which already holds a majority 53% stake in T-Mobile, did not respond to a request for comment on Wednesday morning.  Bloomberg first reported ‌the potential deal.

headline: AT&T adds more wireless subscribers than expected as bundling pays off
summary: AT&T added more wireless subscribers than expected in the first quarter, benefiting from customers opting for the ‌telecom provider's packages bundling wireless and high-speed fiber services.  In a highly competitive ​market, network providers have extended device subsidies, added plan discounts and increased investments in network infrastructure to attract and retain customers.  About 42% of AT&T households that use home internet services  also opted for wireless plans, a ‌convergence dynamic that analysts ⁠had flagged as a key differentiator.

content.title: AT&T adds more wireless subscribers than expected as bundling pays off
content.summary: AT&T added more wireless subscribers than expected in the first quarter, benefiting from customers opting for the ‌telecom provider's packages bundling wireless and high-speed fiber services.  In a highly competitive ​market, network providers have extended device subsidies, added plan discounts and increased investments in network infrastructure to attract and retain customers.  About 42% of AT&T households that use home internet services  also opted for wireless plans, a ‌convergence dynamic that analysts ⁠had flagged as a key differentiator.

headline: Deutsche Telekom exploring merger with T-Mobile, Bloomberg News reports
summary:
STORY: Deutsche Telekom is considering a full combination with T-Mobile US...

and it could result in the largest public M&A deal on record.

Bloomberg News reported the story on Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

The German telecoms group has been in talks about creating a new holding company...

that would make a stock bid for shares of both Deutsche Telekom and T-Mobile.

Deutsche Telekom is the majority shareholder in T-Mobile.

According to LSEG data it has a stake of nearly 53%.

Shares of T-Mobile, which has a market value of about $218 billion, rose more than 1% following the news.

Deutsche Telekom has a valuation of about $166 billion.

In February, the company said it had no plans to sell any T-Mobile shares in 2026.

The potential merger could create the world's biggest wireless operator by market capitalization.

The report added that discussions are at a preliminary stage and any transaction would require political support to move ahead.

Deutsche Telekom and T-Mobile declined to comment when contacted by Reuters.

The White House and the Federal Network Agency, the primary regulatory body overseeing telecommunications in Germany, did not immediately respond to requests.

headline: T-Mobile Stock Falls on Report of Possible Deutsche Telekom Combination
summary: Deutsche Telekom  is weighing a merger with  T-Mobile US  according to a report on Tuesday.  Shares of the German telecommunications provider closed down 2.6% after the Bloomberg report.  Shares of Bellevue, Washington-based T-Mobile fell 1.5%, closing at $195.39 but was up 0.4% in the Wednesday premarket.

content.title: T-Mobile Stock Falls on Report of Possible Deutsche Telekom Combination

Shares of the German telecommunications provider closed down 2.6% after the Bloomberg report.  Shares of Bellevue, Washington-based T-Mobile fell 1.5%, closing at $195.39 but was up 0.4% in the Wednesday premarket.

headline: Deutsche Telekom, T-Mobile in early talks for potential mega merger, sources say
summary: Deutsche Telekom is exploring a deal to combine with its U.S. unit, T-Mobile US , to create a $400 ‌billion telecoms giant in what would be the largest ever public merger, two people ‌familiar with the matter said.  The German group's shares fell about 3% on Wednesday morning  after news of the early-stage talks, first ​reported by Bloomberg.  Telekom already owns a 53% stake in T-Mobile.
